hypothesis: Fine-tuning pre-trained ML interatomic potentials (such as eSEN or eqV2) on stress tensors computed along DFT-converged SSNEB pathways using active learning will disproportionately reduce stress prediction errors relative to energy and force errors, and this targeted stress correction will eliminate the symmetry-breaking artifacts currently observed during VASP restart steps, thereby achieving full DFT-equivalent accuracy in ML-only SSNEB calculations without any DFT restart and extending the computational speedup beyond the current 2–3-fold factor to greater than 10-fold for complex low-symmetry phase transitions such as the CsPbI3 β-to-γ transformation.
